source.manual: MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 3 Part I

description: Material specifications for pavement layers from MoWT Appendix A and Table 5.1. Includes unbound granular, cemented, bituminous, and selected fill materials.

crushed_stone_base.notes: Angular particles, not excessively flaky. Fines should be non-plastic.

natural_gravel_base.cbr_test_condition: Soaked, at probable in-situ density and moisture content
natural_gravel_base.notes: Lateritic, calcareous, quartzitic, river gravels commonly used in Uganda. Marginal materials (PI>6 or CBR borderline) may be acceptable for T1-T2 traffic with local evidence.

granular_subbase.notes: In wet areas, soaked CBR required. In drier areas, unsoaked may be considered with engineer approval.

selected_fill_capping.cbr_test_condition: 93% mod. AASHTO MDD at highest anticipated moisture content
selected_fill_capping.notes: Homogeneous, relatively insensitive to moisture change. Required for S1, S2, S3 subgrades.

cement_treated_base.description: Treatment or stabilisation with CEM I (Section A.3.1)
cement_treated_base.suitable_pi: Preferably < 10. High PI materials should be lime-treated first.
cement_treated_base.notes: Upper and lower UCS bounds set to minimise cracking. Higher cement = wider cracks = worse performance.

lime_treated.suitable_for: High PI materials (PI > 10) that won't respond to cement
lime_treated.notes: Effective for reducing PI. Can be pre-treatment before cement stabilisation.

bitumen_emulsion_treated.notes: Effective for enhancing roadbuilding characteristics. Immune to cracking problems of cement treatment.

AC20.use: Primarily binder course; wearing course in severely loaded areas

surface_dressing.description: Surface dressing / chip seal (Section 6.2, Appendix A.4.2)

gravel_wearing_course.description: Gravel wearing course for unsealed roads

plasticity_index.notes: For tropical areas. Too low = ravels; too high = slippery when wet

gravel_wearing_course.notes: PI range 10-25 specific to tropical conditions. Temperate regions may use different limits.

compaction_standards.mod_aashto: Modified AASHTO T-180 (heavy compaction, 4.5 kg rammer)
compaction_standards.std_proctor: Standard Proctor T-99 (used for subgrade CBR per Table 3.2)
compaction_standards.note: Table 3.2 specifies Standard Proctor for subgrade CBR, Modified AASHTO for base/subbase. Critical distinction.
